requirements.full_sentence:
These Directions shall be called the Reserve Bank of India (Local Area Banks-
Credit Facilities) Directions, 2025.
requirements.object: Bank of India (Local Area Banks- Credit Facilities) Directions, 2025

requirements.full_sentence:
These Directions shall come into effect immediately upon its issuance, unless
indicated otherwise.
requirements.object: upon its issuance

requirements.full_sentence:
These Directions shall be applicable to Local Area Banks (hereinafter collectively
referred to as 'LABs' / 'banks' and individually as a 'LAB' / 'bank').
requirements.object: Area Banks (hereinafter collectively referred to as 'LABs' / 'banks' and individually as a 'LAB' / 'bank')

requirements.full_sentence:
(1) For the purpose of these Directions, following definitions shall apply:
definition of 'income generating loan' as defined subsequently.
requirements.object: generating loan' as defined subsequently

requirements.full_sentence:
(i) 'Annual Percentage Rate' (APR) means the same as defined under the
Reserve Bank of India (Local Area Banks- Responsible Business Conduct)
Directions, 2025.
requirements.object: under the Reserve Bank of India (Local Area Banks- Responsible Business Conduct) Directions, 2025

requirements.full_sentence:
(ii) 'Beneficiary' means the party in whose favour the NFB facility is issued by
a bank.
requirements.object: favour the NFB facility is issued by a bank

requirements.full_sentence:
(iii) 'Bullet Repayment Loans' means loans where both principal and interest
are due for payment at the maturity of the loan.
requirements.object: and interest are due for payment at the maturity of the loan

requirements.full_sentence:
(iv) 'Co-acceptance of bills' means an undertaking to make payment to the
drawer of the bill (seller / exporter) on due date if the buyer / importer fails
to make the payment on that date.
requirements.object: payment to the drawer of the bill (seller / exporter) on due date if the buyer / importer fails to make the payment on that dat

requirements.full_sentence:
(v) 'Collateral Security' or 'Collateral' means an existing asset of the borrower
pledged to the lender for availing and securing a credit facility extended by
the lender to the borrower.
requirements.object: the borrower pledged to the lender for availing and securing a credit facility extended by the lender to the borrower

requirements.full_sentence: (vi) 'Consumption Loan' means any permissible loan that does not fit the
requirements.object: does not fit the

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall put in place Board approved credit policy covering, inter alia, the areas
specified below, to the extent such activities are undertaken by it.
requirements.object: approved credit policy covering, inter alia, the areas specified below, to the extent such activities are undertaken by it

requirements.full_sentence:
Due diligence requirements with respect to LSPs
(1) Digital lending by a bank involving a LSP, shall be carried out under a
contractual agreement between the bank and the LSP, which clearly defines
the respective roles, rights, and obligations of each party thereto.
requirements.object: a contractual agreement between the bank and the LSP, which clearly defines the respective roles, rights, and obligations of each

requirements.full_sentence:
(2) A bank shall conduct enhanced due diligence before they enter into an
agreement with a LSP for digital lending, taking into account LSP's technical
capabilities, robustness of data privacy policies and storage systems, fairness
in conduct with borrowers, past records of conduct and ability to comply with
all applicable regulations and statutes.
requirements.object: before they enter into an agreement with a LSP for digital lending, taking into account LSP's technical capabilities, r

requirements.full_sentence:
(3) A bank shall carry out periodic review of the conduct of the LSP vis-à-vis the
terms of the contractual agreement and shall take appropriate action in the
event of any deviation therefrom.
requirements.object: of the conduct of the LSP vis-à-vis the terms of the contractual agreement and shall take appropriate action in the event of

requirements.full_sentence:
(4) A bank shall lay down, as part of its policy, suitable monitoring mechanisms for
the loan portfolios originated with the support of LSPs.
requirements.object: of its policy, suitable monitoring mechanisms for the loan portfolios originated with the support of LSPs

requirements.full_sentence:
(5) A bank shall impart necessary guidance to LSP acting as a recovery agent, to
discharge their duties responsibly and ensure that LSP complies with the
applicable instructions Reserve Bank of India (Local Area Banks - Responsible
Business Conduct) Directions, 2025.
requirements.object: LSP acting as a recovery agent, to discharge their duties responsibly and ensure that LSP complies with the applicable in

requirements.full_sentence:
(6) A bank shall continue to conform to the extant guidelines on outsourcing
Reserve Bank of India (Local Area Banks - Managing Risks in Outsourcing)
Directions, 2025 and shall ensure that the LSPs engaged by them and the
DLAs (either of the bank or of the LSPs engaged by the LAB) comply with these
Directions.
requirements.object: the extant guidelines on outsourcing Reserve Bank of India (Local Area Banks - Managing Risks in Outsourcing) Directions, 2025

requirements.full_sentence:
(7) As an overarching principle, any outsourcing agreement entered into by a bank
with an LSP shall in no manner dilute or absolve the bank of its obligations
under any statutory or regulatory provision, and the bank shall remain fully
responsible and liable for all acts and omissions of the LSP.
requirements.object: or absolve the bank of its obligations under any statutory or regulatory provision, and the bank shall remain fully responsible an

conditions: where a LSP has agreements with multiple lenders for digital lending
requirements.full_sentence:
Bank-LSP arrangements involving multiple lenders
In cases where a LSP has agreements with multiple lenders for digital lending, a
bank shall ensure the following:
(1) LSP shall provide a digital view of all the loan offers matching the borrower's
request on the DLA which meets the requirement of the borrower.
requirements.object: LSP shall provide a digital view of all the loan offers matching the borrower's request on the DLA which meets the requireme

requirements.full_sentence:
The name
of the unmatched lenders shall also be disclosed in the digital view.
requirements.object: the digital view

requirements.full_sentence:
(2) While the LSP may adopt any mechanism to match the request of borrowers
with the lender(s) to offer a loan, it shall follow a consistent approach for
similarly placed borrowers and products.
requirements.object: for similarly placed borrowers and products

requirements.full_sentence:
The mechanism adopted by the LSP
and any subsequent changes to this mechanism shall be properly
documented.

(3) The digital view of loan offers from matching lenders shall include the name(s)
of the lender(s) extending the loan offer, amount and tenor of loan, APR,
monthly repayment obligation and penal charges (if applicable), in a way which
enables the borrower to make a fair comparison between various offers.
requirements.object: the lender(s) extending the loan offer, amount and tenor of loan, APR, monthly repayment obligation and penal charges (if appli

requirements.full_sentence:
A link
to the KFS shall also be provided in respect of each of the lender.
requirements.object: respect of each of the lender

requirements.full_sentence:
(4) The content displayed by the LSP shall be unbiased, objective and shall not
directly/ indirectly promote or push a product of a particular lender, including
the use of dark patterns / deceptive patterns designed to mislead borrowers
into choosing a particular loan offer.
requirements.object: push a product of a particular lender, including the use of dark patterns / deceptive patterns designed to mislead bor

requirements.full_sentence:
However, ranking of loan offers based on
a publicly pre-disclosed metric for such ranking shall not be construed as
promoting a particular product.
requirements.object: a particular product

requirements.full_sentence:
Note: Dark patterns shall have the same meaning as defined under Section
2(e) of the 'Guidelines for Prevention and Regulation of Dark Patterns, 2023'
dated November 30, 2023, issued by Central Consumer Protection Authority,
and as amended from time to time.
requirements.object: as defined under Section 2(e) of the 'Guidelines for Prevention and Regulation of Dark Patterns, 2023' dated November 30, 2023,

requirements.full_sentence:
Assessing the borrower's creditworthiness
(1) A bank shall obtain the necessary information relating to economic profile of
the borrower with a view to assessing the borrower's creditworthiness before
extending any loan, including, at a minimum, age, occupation and income
details.
requirements.object: relating to economic profile of the borrower with a view to assessing the borrower's creditworthiness before extendin

requirements.full_sentence: The same shall be kept on record for audit purposes.
requirements.object: for audit purposes

requirements.full_sentence:
(2) A bank shall ensure that there is no automatic increase in credit limit unless an
explicit request is received, evaluated and kept on record from the borrower for
such increase.
requirements.object: no automatic increase in credit limit unless an explicit request is received, evaluated and kept on record from the borrower for

requirements.full_sentence:
Disclosures to borrowers
(1) A bank shall provide a Key Fact Statement (KFS), as per instructions contained
in Reserve Bank of India (Local Area Banks - Responsible Business Conduct)
Directions, 2025.
requirements.object: Statement (KFS), as per instructions contained in Reserve Bank of India (Local Area Banks - Responsible Business Conduct) Direction

requirements.full_sentence:
(2) As regards penal charges, a bank shall be guided by Reserve Bank of India
(Local Area Banks - Responsible Business Conduct) Directions, 2025.
requirements.object: Bank of India (Local Area Banks - Responsible Business Conduct) Directions, 2025

requirements.full_sentence:
(3) A bank shall ensure that digitally signed documents (on the letter head of the
bank) viz., KFS, summary of loan product, sanction letter, terms and
conditions, account statements, privacy policies of the bank / LSP with respect
to storage and usage of borrowers' data, etc.
requirements.object: documents (on the letter head of the bank) viz

requirements.full_sentence:
shall automatically flow to the
borrower on the registered and verified email/ SMS upon execution of the loan
contract/ transactions.
requirements.object: borrower on the registered and verified email/ SMS upon execution of the loan contract/ transactions

requirements.full_sentence:
Note: Digitally signed documents shall be in compliance with the provisions of
the Information Technology Act, 2000, as amended from time to time.
requirements.object: the provisions of the Information Technology Act, 2000, as amended from time to time

requirements.full_sentence:
(4) A bank shall maintain a website of their own in public domain, which shall be
kept up to date, inter-alia, with the following details at a prominent single place
on the website for ease of accessibility:
requirements.object: their own in public domain, which shall be kept up to date, inter-alia, with the following details at a prominent single place o

requirements.full_sentence:
Loan disbursal, servicing and repayment
(1) Disbursement of loan by a bank shall always be made into the bank account
of the borrower except for disbursals covered exclusively under statutory or
regulatory mandate (of the Reserve Bank or of any other regulator), and
disbursals for specific end use, provided the loan is disbursed directly into the
bank account of the end-beneficiary.
requirements.object: the bank account of the borrower except for disbursals covered exclusively under statutory or regulatory mandate (of the Reserve Bank or of any other regulator), and disbursals for specific end use

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall ensure that in no case,
disbursal is made to a third-party account, including the accounts of LSP,
except as provided for in this Chapter.
requirements.object: case, disbursal is made to a third-party account, including the accounts of LSP

conditions:
where the loan is disbursed directly to
the bank account of the borrower
requirements.full_sentence:
Provided that advances against salary, where the loan is disbursed directly to
the bank account of the borrower, but the repayment is from the corporate
employer, can be allowed subject to the condition that the loan is repaid by the
corporate employer by deducting the amount from the borrower's salary.

However, it must be ensured that LSPs do not have any control over the flow
of funds directly or indirectly in such transactions and that repayment is directly
from the bank account of the employer to the bank.
requirements.object: do not have any control over the flow of funds directly or indirectly in such transactions and that repayment is directly from th

requirements.full_sentence: (2) A bank shall ensure that all loan servicing, repayment, etc.
requirements.object: servicing, repayment, etc

requirements.full_sentence:
(3) The flow of funds between the bank accounts of the borrower and a bank shall
not be controlled either directly or indirectly by a third-party, including the LSP.
requirements.object: or indirectly by a third-party, including the LSP

requirements.full_sentence: (4) A bank shall ensure that any fees, charges, reimbursements, etc.
requirements.object: charges, reimbursements, etc

requirements.full_sentence:
(5) In case of delinquent loans, a bank may deploy physical interface to recover
loans in cash, wherever necessary.
requirements.object: recover loans in cash

requirements.full_sentence:
However, any recovery by cash shall
be duly reflected in full in the borrower's account on the same day and bank
shall ensure that any fees, charges, etc., payable to LSPs for such recovery
are paid directly by the bank and are not charged by LSP to the borrower either
directly or indirectly from the recovery proceeds.
requirements.object: full in the borrower's account on the same day and bank shall ensure that any fees, charges, etc

requirements.full_sentence:
Cooling-off period
(1) The borrower shall be given an explicit option to exit a digital loan by paying
the principal and the proportionate APR without any penalty during an initial
"cooling-off period".
requirements.object: option to exit a digital loan by paying the principal and the proportionate APR without any penalty during an initial "cooling-of

requirements.full_sentence:
The cooling off period shall be determined by the bank in
terms of their credit policy, subject to the period so determined not being less
than one day.
requirements.object: bank in terms of their credit policy

requirements.full_sentence:
For borrower continuing with the loan even after cooling-off
period, pre-payment shall continue to be allowed as per Reserve Bank of India
(Local Area Banks - Responsible Business Conduct) Directions, 2025.
requirements.object: as per Reserve Bank of India (Local Area Banks - Responsible Business Conduct) Directions, 2025

requirements.full_sentence:
(2) A bank may retain a reasonable one-time processing fee, if the customer exits
the loan during the cooling-off period.
requirements.object: processing fee

requirements.full_sentence:
This, if applicable, shall be disclosed to
the customer upfront in KFS.
requirements.object: customer upfront in KFS

requirements.full_sentence:
Grievance redressal
(1) A bank, and their LSP which has an interface with the borrower, shall designate
nodal grievance redressal officers to deal with digital lending related
complaints/ issues raised by the borrower.
requirements.object: officers to deal with digital lending related complaints/ issues raised by the borrower

requirements.full_sentence:
(2) Contact details of the nodal grievance redressal officers shall be prominently
displayed on the websites of the bank, its LSP and on the DLA, as well as in
the KFS provided to the borrower.
requirements.object: the websites of the bank, its LSP and on the DLA, as well as in the KFS provided to the borrower

requirements.full_sentence:
(3) The facility of lodging complaint shall also be made available on the DLA and
on the website as stated above.
requirements.object: on the DLA and on the website as stated above

requirements.full_sentence:
It is reiterated that responsibility of grievance
redressal shall continue to remain with the bank.
requirements.object: the bank

requirements.full_sentence: This information shall be suitably conveyed to the borrower.
requirements.object: the borrower

requirements.full_sentence:
Collection, usage and sharing of data with third parties
(1) A bank shall ensure that any collection of data by their DLA and DLA of their
LSP is need-based and with prior and explicit consent of the borrower having
audit trail.
requirements.object: of data by their DLA and DLA of their LSP is need-based and with prior and explicit consent of the borrower having audit tr

requirements.full_sentence:
In any case, a bank shall also ensure that DLA of bank / LSP desist
from accessing mobile phone resources like file and media, contact list, call
logs, telephony functions, etc.
requirements.object: of bank / LSP desist from accessing mobile phone resources like file and media, contact list, call logs, telephony functions, etc

requirements.full_sentence:
(2) The borrower shall be provided with an option to give or deny consent for use
of specific data, restrict disclosure to third parties, data retention, revoke
consent already granted to collect personal data and if required, make the bank
/ LSP delete / forget the data.
requirements.object: option to give or deny consent for use of specific data, restrict disclosure to third parties, data retention, revoke consent alre

requirements.full_sentence:
(4) Explicit consent of the borrower shall be taken before sharing personal
information with any third party, except for cases where such sharing is
required as per statutory or regulatory requirement.
requirements.object: personal information with any third party

requirements.full_sentence:
Storage of data
(1) A bank shall ensure that LSP engaged by them do not store personal
information of borrower except some basic minimal data (viz., name, address,
contact details of the customer, etc.) that may be required to carry out their
operations or service within the scope of the bank -LSP agreement.
requirements.object: by them do not store personal information of borrower except some basic minimal data (viz

requirements.full_sentence:
Responsibility regarding data privacy and security of the customer's personal
information on an ongoing basis shall be that of the bank.
requirements.object: bank

requirements.full_sentence:
(2) A bank shall ensure that clear policy guidelines regarding the storage of
customer data including the type of data that can be stored, the length of time
for which data can be stored, restrictions on the use of data, data destruction
protocol, standards for handling security breach, etc., are put in place and also
disclosed by the bank and the LSP engaged by the bank prominently on their
website and DLA at all times.
requirements.object: guidelines regarding the storage of customer data including the type of data that can be stored, the length of time for which

requirements.full_sentence:
(3) A bank shall ensure that no biometric data is stored/ collected by the bank and
LSP, unless allowed under extant statutory guidelines.
requirements.object: data is stored/ collected by the bank and LSP

requirements.full_sentence:
(4) A bank shall ensure that all data is stored only in servers located within India,
while ensuring compliance with statutory obligations/ regulatory instructions.
requirements.object: is stored only in servers located within India, while ensuring compliance with statutory obligations/ regulatory instructions

conditions: in case the data is processed outside India
requirements.full_sentence:
Further, in case the data is processed outside India, the same shall be deleted
from servers outside India and brought back to India within 24 hours of
processing.
requirements.object: outside India and brought back to India within 24 hours of processing

requirements.full_sentence:
Comprehensive privacy policy
(1) A bank and LSPs engaged by the bank shall have a comprehensive privacy
policy compliant with applicable laws, associated regulations and the Reserve
Bank guidelines which shall be made available publicly on the website of bank
and LSP, as the case may be.
requirements.object: policy compliant with applicable laws, associated regulations and the Reserve Bank guidelines which shall be made availab

requirements.full_sentence:
(2) Details of third parties (where applicable) allowed to collect personal
information through the DLA shall also be disclosed in the privacy policy.
requirements.object: the privacy policy

requirements.full_sentence:
Technology standards
(1) A bank shall ensure that they and the LSPs engaged by them comply with
various technology standards/ requirements on cybersecurity stipulated by the
Reserve Bank and other relevant agencies, or as may be specified from time
to time, for undertaking digital lending.
requirements.object: the LSPs engaged by them comply with various technology standards/ requirements on cybersecurity stipulated by the Reserve Bank a

requirements.full_sentence:
CIC Regulations, 2006 and related guidelines
issued by the Reserve Bank from time to time, a bank shall ensure that any
lending done through their DLAs and/ or DLAs of LSPs is reported by them to
CICs irrespective of its nature/ tenor.
requirements.object: done through their DLAs and/ or DLAs of LSPs is reported by them to CICs irrespective of its nature/ tenor

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall ensure that LSPs, if any, associated with such
deferred payment credit products shall abide by the extant outsourcing
guidelines issued by the Reserve Bank and be guided by these Directions.

Reporting of DLAs to RBI
(1) A bank shall report all DLAs deployed/ joined by them, whether their own or
those of the LSPs, either exclusively or as a platform participant, on the
Centralised Information Management System (CIMS) portal of the Reserve
Bank in the requisite format as given in the Annex-I to these Directions.
requirements.object: joined by them, whether their own or those of the LSPs, either exclusively or as a platform participant, on the Centralised

requirements.full_sentence:
(2) A bank shall update the aforesaid list as and when additional DLA(s) are
deployed or the engagement with the existing DLA(s) ceases to exist by filing
the updated data in the CIMS portal.
requirements.object: as and when additional DLA(s) are deployed or the engagement with the existing DLA(s) ceases to exist by filing the updated

requirements.full_sentence:
(3) The Chief Compliance Officer of the bank or any other official designated by
the Board of the bank for the purpose shall certify that the data on DLAs
submitted by them on the CIMS portal is correct and the DLAs are compliant
with all the extant regulatory instructions, including the provisions of this
Chapter.
requirements.object: on DLAs submitted by them on the CIMS portal is correct and the DLAs are compliant with all the extant regulatory instructions,

requirements.full_sentence:
(4) Without prejudice to the generality of the above, the Chief Compliance Officer/
other official designated by the Board of the bank shall certify the following
aspects:

(5) A bank shall ensure the correctness and timeliness of information regarding
DLAs, as the data, as submitted by the bank on CIMS, shall be published on
the website of the Reserve Bank in an automated manner and the Reserve
Bank shall not verify/ validate the data submitted on CIMS.
requirements.object: submitted on CIMS

requirements.full_sentence:
All issues and
grievances of customers concerning DLAs shall be addressed and dealt with
by the bank directly.
requirements.object: with by the bank directly

requirements.full_sentence:
(6) A bank shall ensure that the inclusion of any third party DLAs deployed by them
as part of above reporting, shall not be construed by the DLAs or any
associated entity as conferring any form of registration, authorization, or
endorsement by the Reserve Bank.
requirements.object: DLAs or any associated entity as conferring any form of registration, authorization, or endorsement by the Reserve Bank

requirements.full_sentence:
bank shall also ensure that such inclusion
is not misrepresented in any marketing, promotional, or other materials issued
by or on behalf of the DLAs.
requirements.object: inclusion is not misrepresented in any marketing, promotional, or other materials issued by or on behalf of the DLAs

requirements.full_sentence:
Eligibility as Default Loss Guarantee (DLG) provider
(1) A bank may enter into DLG arrangements only with an LSP/ other lender
engaged as an LSP.
requirements.object: only with an LSP/ other lender engaged as an LSP

requirements.full_sentence:
Further, the LSP providing DLG shall be incorporated as
a company under the Companies Act, 2013.
requirements.object: company under the Companies Act, 2013

conditions: if any, payable to/ received by the DLG provider
requirements.full_sentence:
Due diligence and other requirements with respect to DLG provider
(1) A bank, including a bank acting as DLG provider, shall lay down, as part of its
policy, the eligibility criteria for DLG provider, nature and extent of DLG cover,
process of monitoring and reviewing the DLG arrangement, and the details of
the fees, if any, payable to/ received by the DLG provider, as the case may be,
before entering into any DLG arrangement.
requirements.object: of its policy, the eligibility criteria for DLG provider, nature and extent of DLG cover, process of monitoring and reviewing the DLG arrangement, and the details of the fees

requirements.full_sentence:
(2) A bank shall ensure that any DLG arrangement does not act as a substitute for
credit appraisal requirements and robust credit underwriting standards need to
be put in place irrespective of the DLG cover.
requirements.object: arrangement does not act as a substitute for credit appraisal requirements and robust credit underwriting standards need to be put

requirements.full_sentence:
(3) Every time a bank enters into or renews a DLG arrangement, it shall obtain
adequate information to satisfy itself that the entity extending DLG would be
able to honour it.
requirements.object: satisfy itself that the entity extending DLG would be able to honour it

requirements.full_sentence:
Such information shall, at a minimum, include a declaration
from the DLG provider, certified by the statutory auditor of the DLG provider,
on the aggregate DLG amount outstanding, the number of lenders and the
respective number of portfolios against which DLG has been provided.
requirements.object: include a declaration from the DLG provider, certified by the statutory auditor of the DLG provider, on the aggregate DLG amount outst

requirements.full_sentence:
The
declaration shall also contain past default rates on similar portfolios.
requirements.object: rates on similar portfolios

requirements.full_sentence:
Restrictions on entering into DLG arrangements
(1) A bank shall not enter into DLG arrangements for revolving credit facilities
offered through digital lending channel.
requirements.object: for revolving credit facilities offered through digital lending channel

requirements.full_sentence:
(2) A bank shall not enter into DLG arrangements on the loans which are covered
by the credit guarantee schemes administered by trust funds as specified
under Reserve Bank of India (Local Area Banks- Prudential Norms on Capital
Adequacy) Directions, 2025.
requirements.object: on the loans which are covered by the credit guarantee schemes administered by trust funds as specified under Reserve Bank

requirements.full_sentence:
Structure of DLG arrangements
(1) DLG arrangements shall be backed by an explicit and legally enforceable
contract between a bank and the DLG provider.
requirements.object: explicit and legally enforceable contract between a bank and the DLG provider

requirements.full_sentence:
Such contract, among other
things, shall contain the following details:

Forms of DLG
(1) A bank shall accept DLG only in one or more of the following forms:
requirements.object: one or more of the following forms:

requirements.full_sentence:
Cap on DLG
(1) A bank shall ensure that the total amount of DLG cover on any outstanding
portfolio which is specified upfront shall not exceed five per cent of the total
amount disbursed out of that loan portfolio at any given time.
requirements.object: of the total amount disbursed out of that loan portfolio at any given time

conditions:
In case of implicit
guarantee arrangements
requirements.full_sentence:
In case of implicit
guarantee arrangements, the DLG Provider shall not bear performance risk of
more than the equivalent amount of five per cent of the underlying loan
portfolio.
requirements.object: more than the equivalent amount of five per cent of the underlying loan portfolio

requirements.full_sentence:
(2) The portfolio over which DLG can be offered shall consist of identifiable and
measurable loan assets which have been sanctioned (the 'DLG set').
requirements.object: measurable loan assets which have been sanctioned (the 'DLG set')

requirements.full_sentence:
This
portfolio shall remain fixed for the purpose of DLG cover and is not meant to
be dynamic.
requirements.object: purpose of DLG cover and is not meant to be dynamic

requirements.full_sentence:
This
portfolio shall remain "frozen" for the purpose of the specific DLG arrangement
- meaning that no loan assets can be added or removed from it, except through
loan repayment/ write-off.
requirements.object: purpose of the specific DLG arrangement - meaning that no loan assets can be added or removed from it

requirements.full_sentence:
The ceiling for DLG cover on such portfolio shall be fixed at ₹2 crore (5 per
cent of ₹40 crore), which shall get activated proportionately as and when the
loans are disbursed.
requirements.object: crore (5 per cent of ₹40 crore), which shall get activated proportionately as and when the loans are disbursed

requirements.full_sentence:
Then as on April 1, 2024, the DLG cover available for
the portfolio shall be ₹0.5 crore (5 per cent of disbursed).

conditions: if loans of ₹10 crore are further disbursed on April 15
requirements.full_sentence:
Subsequently, if loans of ₹10 crore are further disbursed on April 15, 2024, the
DLG cover shall proportionately increase to ₹1 crore effective April 15, 2024.
requirements.object: crore effective April 15, 2024

requirements.full_sentence:
In this case, the outstanding portfolio in the books of the LAB would be ₹15
crore and the DLG cover shall remain at ₹1 crore.

In this
case, as of Sept 30, 2024 the outstanding portfolio in the books of the bank
shall be ₹15 crore (₹20 crore original portfolio less ₹5 crore loans matured
without default) but no headroom for DLG will be available as the maximum
permissible DLG cover of ₹1 crore (5 per cent of disbursed) has been
exhausted.
requirements.object: crore original portfolio less ₹5 crore loans matured without default) but no headroom for DLG will be available as the maximum permi

requirements.full_sentence:
In such a
case, the amount of the outstanding portfolio in the books of the LAB as on
October 31, 2024 shall come down to ₹14 crore (₹20 crore original portfolio
less ₹5 crore loans matured without any default less ₹1 crore loans which were
in default and recovered).
requirements.object: crore (₹20 crore original portfolio less ₹5 crore loans matured without any default less ₹1 crore loans which were in default and rec

requirements.full_sentence:
Recognition of NPA
(1) Recognition of individual loan assets in the portfolio as Non-Performing Asset
(NPA) and consequent provisioning shall be the responsibility of the bank as
per the Reserve Bank of India (Local Area Banks - Income Recognition, Asset
Classification and Provisioning) Directions, 2025 irrespective of any DLG cover
available at the portfolio level.
requirements.object: the bank as per the Reserve Bank of India (Local Area Banks - Income Recognition, Asset Classification and Provisioning) Dire

requirements.full_sentence:
(2) The amount of DLG invoked shall not be set off against the underlying
individual loans, i.e.
requirements.object: the underlying individual loans, i

requirements.full_sentence:
the liability of the borrowers in respect of the underlying
loan shall remain unaffected.

(4) DLG amount once invoked by a bank shall not be reinstated, including through
loan recovery.
requirements.object: loan recovery

requirements.full_sentence:
Treatment of DLG for regulatory capital
(1) Capital computation, i.e., computation of exposure and application of Credit
Risk Mitigation benefits on individual loan assets in the portfolio shall continue
to be governed by the Reserve Bank of India (Local Area Banks - Prudential
Norms on Capital Adequacy) Directions, 2025.
requirements.object: by the Reserve Bank of India (Local Area Banks - Prudential Norms on Capital Adequacy) Directions, 2025

requirements.full_sentence:
(2) In case, DLG provider is a bank, it shall deduct full amount of the DLG which
is outstanding from its capital.
requirements.object: the DLG which is outstanding from its capital

requirements.full_sentence:
Invocation and tenor of DLG
(1) A bank shall invoke DLG within a maximum overdue period of 120 days, unless
the loan dues are made good by the borrower before that.
requirements.object: maximum overdue period of 120 days

requirements.full_sentence:
(2) The period for which the DLG agreement remains in force shall not be less
than the longest tenor of the loan in the underlying loan portfolio.
requirements.object: longest tenor of the loan in the underlying loan portfolio

requirements.full_sentence:
Disclosure requirements
(1) A bank shall put in place a mechanism to ensure that LSP with whom they
have a DLG arrangement shall publish on their website the total number of
portfolios and the respective amount of each portfolio on which DLG has been
offered.
requirements.object: mechanism to ensure that LSP with whom they have a DLG arrangement shall publish on their website the total number of portfolios and th

requirements.full_sentence:
The name of the lender(s) may or may not be disclosed as part of
disclosure under this provision.
requirements.object: disclosed as part of disclosure under this provision

requirements.full_sentence:
(2) Disclosure under para (1) above shall be made on a monthly basis, with the
disclosure for any given month to be provided no later than seven (7) working
days following the conclusion of that month.
requirements.object: monthly basis, with the disclosure for any given month to be provided no later than seven (7) working days following the conclusion of th

requirements.full_sentence:
Exceptions
Guarantees covered under the following schemes/ entities shall not be covered
within the definition of DLG:
(1) Guarantee schemes of Credit Guarantee Fund Trust for Micro and Small
Enterprises (CGTMSE), Credit Risk Guarantee Fund Trust for Low Income
Housing (CRGFTLIH) and individual schemes under National Credit
Guarantee Trustee Company Ltd (NCGTC).
requirements.object: definition of DLG: (1) Guarantee schemes of Credit Guarantee Fund Trust for Micro and Small Enterprises (CGTMSE), Credit Risk Gu

requirements.full_sentence:
General provisions
(1) The directions of this Chapter shall also be applicable to all loans offered on
Debit Card, including EMI programmes.
requirements.object: all loans offered on Debit Card, including EMI programmes

requirements.full_sentence:
(2) DLG arrangements entered between a bank and LSP conforming to the
instructions laid down in this Chapter shall not attract the provisions of 'loan
participation' as defined under Reserve Bank of India (Local Area Banks -
Transfer and Distribution of Credit Risk) Directions, 2025.
requirements.object: 'loan participation' as defined under Reserve Bank of India (Local Area Banks - Transfer and Distribution of Credit Risk) Di

requirements.full_sentence:
The credit policy (hereinafter called the policy) of a bank, as required in terms of
paragraph 5 of these Directions, shall include, inter alia, appropriate single
borrower limits and aggregate limits for the portfolio of loans against collateral of
jewellery, ornaments or coins made of gold or silver ("eligible collateral" for this
Chapter);
requirements.object: single borrower limits and aggregate limits for the portfolio of loans against collateral of jewellery, ornaments or

requirements.full_sentence:
The policy shall also include appropriate documentation to
be obtained and maintained for loans proposed to be categorised under priority
sector lending.
requirements.object: to be obtained and maintained for loans proposed to be categorised under priority sector lending

requirements.full_sentence: A bank may decide on a suitable approach for lending against eligible collateral as
requirements.object: approach for lending against eligible collateral as

requirements.full_sentence:
However,
detailed credit assessment, including assessment of borrower's repayment
capacity shall be undertaken in case the total loan amount against eligible
collateral is above ₹2.5 lakh to a borrower.
requirements.object: the total loan amount against eligible collateral is above ₹2

conditions: Provided that in case of Bullet repayment loans
requirements.full_sentence:
Provided that in case of Bullet repayment loans, the threshold loan amount for
detailed credit assessment shall be the total amount payable at maturity.
requirements.object: payable at maturity

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank may renew an existing loan or sanction a top-up loan upon a formal request
from the borrower, subject to a credit assessment in accordance with paragraph
requirements.object: or sanction a top-up loan upon a formal request from the borrower

requirements.full_sentence:
Such renewal or top-up shall be permitted only within the permissible LTV, and
provided the loan is classified as standard.
requirements.object: the permissible LTV, and provided the loan is classified as standard

requirements.full_sentence:
Further, renewal of bullet repayment
loan shall be allowed only after payment of accrued interest, if any.
requirements.object: payment of accrued interest

requirements.full_sentence:
The bank shall
ensure that such renewals and top-ups are clearly identifiable in its Core Banking
System or Loan Processing System.
requirements.object: and top-ups are clearly identifiable in its Core Banking System or Loan Processing System

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall not grant any advance or loan:
(1) For purchase of gold in any form including primary gold, ornaments, jewellery,
or coins, or for purchase of financial assets backed by gold, e.g., units of
Exchange-traded funds (ETFs) or units of Mutual Funds;
requirements.object: loan: (1) For purchase of gold in any form including primary gold, ornaments, jewellery, or coins, or for purchase of financial a

requirements.full_sentence: A bank shall not extend a loan where ownership of the collateral is doubtful.
requirements.object: ownership of the collateral is doubtful

requirements.full_sentence:
A
suitable document or declaration shall be obtained from the borrower in all cases
to the effect that the borrower is the rightful owner of the eligible collateral.
requirements.object: borrower in all cases to the effect that the borrower is the rightful owner of the eligible collateral

requirements.full_sentence:
Multiple
or frequent sanction of loans against eligible collateral to the same borrower,
aggregating to a value in excess of a threshold to be decided by the lender, must
be examined closely as part of the transaction monitoring under the anti-money
laundering (AML) framework.
requirements.object: part of the transaction monitoring under the anti-money laundering (AML) framework

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall not:
(1) Avail loans by re-pledging gold or silver pledged to it by its borrowers.
requirements.object: by re-pledging gold or silver pledged to it by its borrowers

requirements.full_sentence:
Tenor of consumption loans in the nature of bullet repayment loans shall be capped
at 12 months, which may be renewed in terms of paragraph 34.
requirements.object: months, which may be renewed in terms of paragraph 34

requirements.full_sentence:
Loans against ornaments and coins shall be subject to the following:
(1) The aggregate weight of ornaments pledged for all loans to a borrower shall not
exceed 1 kilogram for gold ornaments, and 10 kilograms for silver ornaments.
requirements.object: gold ornaments, and 10 kilograms for silver ornaments

requirements.full_sentence:
(2) The aggregate weight of coin(s) pledged for all loans to a borrower shall not
exceed 50 grams in case of gold coins, and 500 grams in case of silver coins.
requirements.object: case of gold coins, and 500 grams in case of silver coins

requirements.full_sentence:
Gold or silver accepted as collateral shall be valued based on the reference price
corresponding to its actual purity (caratage).
requirements.object: the reference price corresponding to its actual purity (caratage)

requirements.full_sentence:
For this purpose, the lower of
(1) the average closing price for gold or silver, as the case may be, of that specific
purity over the preceding 30 days, or
(2) the closing price for gold or silver, as the case may be, of that specific purity
on the preceding day, as published either by the India Bullion and Jewellers
Association Ltd.
requirements.object: purity over the preceding 30 days, or (2) the closing price for gold or silver, as the case may be, of that specific purity on th

requirements.full_sentence:
(IBJA) or by a commodity exchange regulated by the
Securities and Exchange Board of India (SEBI) shall be used.

conditions: If price information for the specific purity is not directly available
requirements.full_sentence:
If price information for the specific purity is not directly available, the lender shall
use the published price available for the nearest available purity and
proportionately adjust the weight of the collateral based on its actual purity to arrive
at valuation.
requirements.object: available for the nearest available purity and proportionately adjust the weight of the collateral based on its actual purity

requirements.full_sentence:
For the purpose of valuation, only the intrinsic value of the gold or silver contained
in the eligible collateral shall be reckoned and no other cost elements, such as
precious stones or gems, shall be added thereto.
requirements.object: other cost elements, such as precious stones or gems, shall be added thereto

requirements.full_sentence:
The maximum LTV ratio in respect of consumption loans against the eligible
collateral shall not exceed LTV ratios as provided in the table below:
Total consumption loan
amount per borrower Maximum LTV ratio
≤ ₹2.5 lakh 85 per cent > ₹2.5 lakh & ≤ ₹5 lakh 80 per cent
> ₹5 lakh 75 per cent
Explanation: 'Loan to Value (LTV) ratio' on a day in this context means the ratio of
the outstanding loan amount to the value of the pledged collateral or security, as
the case may be, on that day.
requirements.object: provided in the table below: Total consumption loan amount per borrower Maximum LTV ratio ≤ ₹2

conditions: In case of bullet repayment loans
requirements.full_sentence:
In case of bullet repayment loans, however, the LTV
calculation, and the loan amount, shall take into account the total amount
repayable at maturity.
requirements.object: total amount repayable at maturity

requirements.full_sentence:
The prescribed LTV ratio shall be maintained on an ongoing basis throughout the
tenor of the loan.
requirements.object: ongoing basis throughout the tenor of the loan

requirements.full_sentence:
For conduct related aspects and collateral management, the bank shall be guided
by the instructions contained in Reserve Bank of India (Local Area Banks-
Responsible Business Conduct) Directions, 2025.
requirements.object: instructions contained in Reserve Bank of India (Local Area Banks- Responsible Business Conduct) Directions, 2025

requirements.full_sentence: The bank shall generally disburse loans into borrower's bank accounts.
requirements.object: borrower's bank accounts

requirements.full_sentence:
Banks
shall comply with the Reserve Bank of India (Local Area Banks- Know Your
Customer) Directions, 2025.
requirements.object: Bank of India (Local Area Banks- Know Your Customer) Directions, 2025

requirements.full_sentence:
Provisions of Sections 269 SS and 269 T of the
Income Tax Act, 1961, and associated rules shall be complied with, as may be
applicable.
requirements.object: may be applicable

requirements.full_sentence:
In case of bank transfers, the bank shall ensure that:
(1) Loan disbursals are made to the borrower's account and not to a third-party
account
Exceptions:
requirements.object: disbursals are made to the borrower's account and not to a third-party account Exceptions:

requirements.full_sentence:
Running multiple loans simultaneously to a single borrower or a group of related
borrowers may be prone to misuse and susceptible to fraud.
requirements.object: and susceptible to fraud

requirements.full_sentence:
Consequently, such
practices shall be subject to stricter internal audit and supervisory examination.
requirements.object: internal audit and supervisory examination

requirements.full_sentence:
For instructions on disclosure requirements, the bank shall be guided by the
instructions contained in Reserve Bank of India (Local Area Banks- Financial
Statements: Presentation and Disclosures) Directions, 2025.
requirements.object: instructions contained in Reserve Bank of India (Local Area Banks- Financial Statements: Presentation and Disclosures) Directions, 20

requirements.full_sentence:
For this purpose, the household shall
mean an individual family unit, i.e., husband, wife and their unmarried children.
requirements.object: unit, i

requirements.full_sentence:
All collateral-free loans, irrespective of end use and mode of application /
processing / disbursal (either through physical or digital channels), provided to low-
income households, i.e., households having annual income up to ₹3,00,000, shall
be considered as microfinance loans.
requirements.object: loans

requirements.full_sentence:
Explanation: To ensure collateral-free nature of the microfinance loan, the loan
shall not be linked with a lien on the deposit account of the borrower or backed by
hypothecation of any security.
requirements.object: lien on the deposit account of the borrower or backed by hypothecation of any security

requirements.full_sentence:
Each bank shall put in place a board-approved policy for assessment of household
income.
requirements.object: board-approved policy for assessment of household income

requirements.full_sentence:
Indicative methodology for assessment of household income is outlined
below:
(1) For undertaking the income assessment of a low-income household,
information related to following parameters may be captured by the lender:
requirements.object: lender:

requirements.full_sentence:
(c) The income assessment as above may be carried out for all earning
members with respect to all sources (primary or secondary) of income.
requirements.object: all earning members with respect to all sources (primary or secondary) of income

requirements.full_sentence:
While assessing income of all members from all sources, it may be
ensured that there is no double counting of income such as counting of
salary income of one migrant member also as remittance income for
the household.
requirements.object: is no double counting of income such as counting of salary income of one migrant member also as remittance income for the househ

requirements.full_sentence:
(d) While the income computation may be done on a monthly basis, the
income assessment for all members and sources may be carried out
over a period of minimum one year to ascertain the stability of the
household income.
requirements.object: monthly basis, the income assessment for all members and sources may be carried out over a period of minimum one year to ascertain the st

requirements.full_sentence:
(b) Irregular expenses over last one year (medical expenses, house
renovation, purchase of household goods, functions, etc.)
(2) Self-reported income at 1(ii) above may be corroborated with the profile of
household at 1(i) and household expenses at 1(iii).
requirements.object: profile of household at 1(i) and household expenses at 1(iii)

requirements.full_sentence:
Further, household income
may also be verified from other sources (bank account statements of the
borrowers, group members, other references in the vicinity, etc.).
requirements.object: other sources (bank account statements of the borrowers, group members, other references in the vicinity, etc

requirements.full_sentence:
Self-regulatory organisations (SROs) and other associations / agencies may also
develop a common framework based on the indicative methodology.
requirements.object: framework based on the indicative methodology

requirements.full_sentence:
The banks
may adopt / modify this framework suitably as per their requirements with approval
of their boards.
requirements.object: framework suitably as per their requirements with approval of their boards

requirements.full_sentence:
Each bank shall mandatorily submit information regarding household income to
the Credit Information Companies (CICs).
requirements.object: household income to the Credit Information Companies (CICs)

requirements.full_sentence:
Reasons for any divergence between
the already reported household income and assessed household income shall be
specifically ascertained from the borrower(s) before updating the assessed
household income with CICs.
requirements.object: the borrower(s) before updating the assessed household income with CICs

requirements.full_sentence:
Each bank shall have a board-approved policy regarding the limit on the outflows
on account of repayment of monthly loan obligations of a household as a
percentage of the monthly household income.
requirements.object: regarding the limit on the outflows on account of repayment of monthly loan obligations of a household as a percentage of

requirements.full_sentence:
This shall be subject to a limit of
maximum 50 per cent of the monthly household income.
requirements.object: limit of maximum 50 per cent of the monthly household income

requirements.full_sentence:
Explanation: Any future expected income from the asset/ activity financed by a
microfinance loan shall not be included in the household income for the purpose
of calculating the indebtedness of the household.
requirements.object: household income for the purpose of calculating the indebtedness of the household

requirements.full_sentence:
The computation of loan repayment obligations shall take into account all
outstanding loans (collateral-free microfinance loans as well as any other type of
collateralized loans) of the household.
requirements.object: outstanding loans (collateral-free microfinance loans as well as any other type of collateralized loans) of the household

requirements.full_sentence:
The outflows capped at 50 per cent of the
monthly household income shall include repayments (including both principal as
well as interest component) towards all existing loans as well as the loan under
consideration.
requirements.object: principal as well as interest component) towards all existing loans as well as the loan under consideration

requirements.full_sentence:
Existing loans, for which outflows on account of repayment of monthly loan
obligations of a household as a percentage of the monthly household income
exceed the limit of 50 per cent, shall be allowed to mature.

However, in such cases,
no new loans shall be provided to these households till the prescribed limit of 50
per cent is complied with.
requirements.object: households till the prescribed limit of 50 per cent is complied with

requirements.full_sentence:
Each bank shall provide timely and accurate data to the CICs and use the data
available with them to ensure compliance with the level of indebtedness.
requirements.object: data to the CICs and use the data available with them to ensure compliance with the level of indebtedness

requirements.full_sentence:
Besides,
the bank shall also ascertain the same from other sources such as declaration from
the borrowers, their bank account statements and local enquiries.
requirements.object: from other sources such as declaration from the borrowers, their bank account statements and local enquiries

requirements.full_sentence:
The bank shall have a board-approved policy to provide the flexibility of repayment
periodicity on microfinance loans as per borrowers' requirement.
requirements.object: to provide the flexibility of repayment periodicity on microfinance loans as per borrowers' requirement

requirements.full_sentence:
The bank providing microfinance loans shall refer to:
(1) Reserve Bank of India (Local Area Banks - Interest Rates on Advances)
Directions, 2025 for pricing of loans.
requirements.object: Bank of India (Local Area Banks - Interest Rates on Advances) Directions, 2025 for pricing of loans

requirements.full_sentence:
Within this Chapter, the term
Regulated Entity (RE) or Regulated Entities (REs) shall refer to:
(1) Commercial Banks (including Regional Rural Banks and Local Area Banks).
requirements.object: Banks (including Regional Rural Banks and Local Area Banks)

requirements.full_sentence:
The Directions in this Chapter shall not apply to the derivative exposures of the
bank (wherever permitted under specific regulatory framework issued by RBI),
other than the general conditions as laid down in this Chapter.
requirements.object: exposures of the bank (wherever permitted under specific regulatory framework issued by RBI), other than the general condition

requirements.full_sentence:
The credit policy of the bank shall incorporate suitable provisions for issue of NFB
facilities, inter alia, covering aspects relating to type of NFB facilities, limits granted,
credit appraisal, security requirement, fraud prevention, overall monitoring
mechanism including post-sanction monitoring, delegation matrix, audit and
internal controls, compliance to uniform standards issued by standard setting
bodies and other safeguards.
requirements.object: issue of NFB facilities, inter alia, covering aspects relating to type of NFB facilities, limits granted, credit a

requirements.full_sentence:
The bank shall issue a NFB facility only on behalf of a customer having funded credit
facility from the bank.
requirements.object: only on behalf of a customer having funded credit facility from the bank

requirements.full_sentence:
Provided that this clause shall not be applicable in respect of:
(1) Derivative contracts entered into by the bank with counterparty.
requirements.object: of: (1) Derivative contracts entered into by the bank with counterparty

requirements.full_sentence:
Explanation: The eligible financial collateral specified herein shall be as defined
under Reserve Bank of India (Commercial Banks - Prudential Norms on
Capital Adequacy) Directions, 2025.
requirements.object: Reserve Bank of India (Commercial Banks - Prudential Norms on Capital Adequacy) Directions, 2025

requirements.full_sentence:
The bank shall not issue a NFB facility to any entity assuring redemption /
repayment of funds raised by any entity via deposits, issuance of bonds, or in any
other form, unless specifically permitted under any regulatory guidelines /
directions issued by the Reserve Bank.
requirements.object: to any entity assuring redemption / repayment of funds raised by any entity via deposits, issuance of bonds, or in any other form

requirements.full_sentence:
Once a NFB facility devolves and is converted into a fund based facility, then the
prudential norms shall be as applicable to fund based facilities.
requirements.object: fund based facilities

requirements.full_sentence:
In general, a guarantee (or a counter-guarantee) issued by the bank (guarantor)
shall be irrevocable (i.e., there shall be no clause in the contract that would allow the
guarantor to unilaterally cancel the same), unconditional (i.e.

there shall be no
clause in the contract that could prevent the bank from being obliged to pay out in a
timely manner in the event that the original counterparty fails to meet its obligation),
incontrovertible and shall contain a clear mechanism for honouring the same
without demur as and when invoked.
requirements.object: the contract that could prevent the bank from being obliged to pay out in a timely manner in the event that the original counterparty

requirements.full_sentence:
The bank shall put in place suitable internal aggregate / individual ceilings for
issuance of guarantees in general and unsecured guarantees in particular.
requirements.object: internal aggregate / individual ceilings for issuance of guarantees in general and unsecured guarantees in particular

requirements.full_sentence:
Provided that the total volume of guaranteed obligations of LABs outstanding at
any time shall not exceed 5 per cent of their total assets as per the previous
financial year's balance sheet.
requirements.object: of their total assets as per the previous financial year's balance sheet

requirements.full_sentence:
Further, unsecured guarantees of LABs shall be
restricted to 1.25 per cent of total assets.

Any such LAB in breach of the above
stipulation as on the date of issue of these Directions shall meet the above
threshold by April 01, 2027.
requirements.object: by April 01, 2027

requirements.full_sentence:
The provisions of the internal policy relating to guarantees shall, inter alia, address
aspects related to invocation and settlement mechanism, claim period, tenor, fee /
commission / applicable charges, timelines for release of security, renewal, fraud
prevention measures etc.
requirements.object: aspects related to invocation and settlement mechanism, claim period, tenor, fee / commission / applicable charges, timelines fo

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall honour the guarantee issued by it as and when invoked in accordance
with the terms and conditions of the guarantee deed unless there is court order
restraining the same.
requirements.object: by it as and when invoked in accordance with the terms and conditions of the guarantee deed unless there is court order re

requirements.full_sentence:
Wherever a bank issues an electronic Guarantee, it shall frame a standard
operating procedure (SOP) aimed at minimization of manual intervention;
requirements.object: procedure (SOP) aimed at minimization of manual intervention

requirements.full_sentence:
The SOP shall, inter alia, consider the aspects mentioned from paragraph 73
to 77 of these Directions.
requirements.object: the aspects mentioned from paragraph 73 to 77 of these Directions

requirements.full_sentence:
Policy and SOP for issuance of Electronic Guarantee
(1) The bank shall have suitable enabling provisions in its credit policy which shall,
inter alia, envisage the adoption of electronic Guarantees, the risk controls to
be put in place, delegation of authority, the monitoring process, etc.
requirements.object: in its credit policy which shall, inter alia, envisage the adoption of electronic Guarantees, the risk controls to b

requirements.full_sentence:
(2) The bank shall put in place appropriate SOPs for user reference, detailing all
the steps to be followed during the entire electronic Guarantee lifecycle.
requirements.object: SOPs for user reference, detailing all the steps to be followed during the entire electronic Guarantee lifecycle

requirements.full_sentence:
Electronic Guarantees shall not be issued without ensuring that the underlying
transaction has been duly reflected in the Core Banking System (CBS)/ Trade
Finance System (TFS).
requirements.object: that the underlying transaction has been duly reflected in the Core Banking System (CBS)/ Trade Finance System (TFS)
environment covering the policies, processes and systems

requirements.full_sentence:
(2) The bank shall ensure that all features relating to the entire lifecycle events of
electronic Guarantees such as issuance, amendment, invocation, cancellation
etc.
requirements.object: relating to the entire lifecycle events of electronic Guarantees such as issuance, amendment, invocation, cancellation etc

requirements.full_sentence:
shall be available on its platform through suitable integration with the
electronic Guarantee service provider.
requirements.object: platform through suitable integration with the electronic Guarantee service provider

requirements.full_sentence:
(3) The CBS / TFS shall be integrated with the APIs and other related messaging
platforms offered by the electronic Guarantee service provider, in Straight
Through Processing (STP) mode, without any manual intervention.
requirements.object: APIs and other related messaging platforms offered by the electronic Guarantee service provider, in Straight Through Processing

requirements.full_sentence:
User Roles for issuance of Electronic Guarantee
(1) The bank shall have an efficient system of 'Maker, Checker and Authorizer' for
issuance and monitoring of electronic Guarantees, while ensuring strict access
control and an effective segregation of the role and accountability.
requirements.object: of 'Maker, Checker and Authorizer' for issuance and monitoring of electronic Guarantees, while ensuring strict access control

requirements.full_sentence:
(2) No role involved in electronic Guarantee issuance lifecycle shall violate
principle of segregation of duties, four / six eye principle and no employee shall
be allocated roles / privileges across systems, applications that are conflicting
in nature or in violation of four/ six eye principle.
requirements.object: of duties, four / six eye principle and no employee shall be allocated roles / privileges across systems, application

requirements.full_sentence:
(3) The system access shall be provided only to specified users, and access
through generic user IDs shall not be permitted.

User review shall be
continuous, at defined periodicity and identifiable at any point of time with
respective rights and privileges.
requirements.object: periodicity and identifiable at any point of time with respective rights and privileges

requirements.full_sentence:
The user privileges shall be decided on "need
to know/ need to do" basis.
requirements.object: to know/ need to do" basis

requirements.full_sentence:
Control Measures for issuance of Electronic Guarantee
(1) The bank shall have in place a system of periodical review and reconciliation
of all the electronic Guarantees issued / modified / cancelled, during the
specified period.
requirements.object: system of periodical review and reconciliation of all the electronic Guarantees issued / modified / cancelled, during the specified pe

requirements.full_sentence:
(2) The issuance of electronic Guarantees shall be mandatorily covered within the
scope of concurrent audit and RBIA of the bank.
requirements.object: the scope of concurrent audit and RBIA of the bank

requirements.full_sentence:
Other aspects pertaining to issuance of Electronic Guarantee
(1) The robustness of the electronic Guarantee systems shall be part of the
Vulnerability Assessment / Penetration Testing (VA / PT), Information Systems
Audit.
requirements.object: Vulnerability Assessment / Penetration Testing (VA / PT), Information Systems Audit

requirements.full_sentence: (2) Dependence on the vendors for day-to-day transactions shall be avoided.

Access to production systems shall be provided to vendors only in a controlled
environment, and audit trail shall be maintained.
requirements.object: only in a controlled environment, and audit trail shall be maintained

requirements.full_sentence:
(3) Security Incident and Event Management (SIEM) tool shall be integrated with
the concerned servers and consoles / PCs connected to electronic Guarantee
related critical systems directly in its VLAN to generate automatic alerts.
requirements.object: concerned servers and consoles / PCs connected to electronic Guarantee related critical systems directly in its VLAN to generat

requirements.full_sentence:
(4) The bank shall integrate electronic Guarantee systems with Privileged User
Management Systems / Identity and Access Management systems.
requirements.object: with Privileged User Management Systems / Identity and Access Management systems

requirements.full_sentence:
The logs of
the same shall be monitored through Security Operation Centre (SOC) setup.
requirements.object: Operation Centre (SOC) setup

requirements.full_sentence:
(5) Business Continuity Measures and contingency plans for system failures, shall
be put in place by the bank.
requirements.object: by the bank

requirements.full_sentence:
The bank shall not provide a guarantee favouring another RE to enable it to provide
any fund based credit facility to an obligor unless specifically permitted by RBI.
requirements.object: another RE to enable it to provide any fund based credit facility to an obligor unless specifically permitted by RBI

requirements.full_sentence:
Provided that this clause shall not be applicable in case of credit facilities extended
against guarantees pertaining to trade related transactions.
requirements.object: of credit facilities extended against guarantees pertaining to trade related transactions

requirements.full_sentence:
However, the bank may provide a guarantee favouring another RE for a NFB facility
extended by the latter.
requirements.object: another RE for a NFB facility extended by the latter

requirements.full_sentence:
Such guarantee issued by a RE shall be treated as an
exposure on the obligor on whose behalf the guarantee has been issued by it, for
all purposes including for the calculation of capital adequacy.
requirements.object: exposure on the obligor on whose behalf the guarantee has been issued by it, for all purposes including for the calculation of capita

requirements.full_sentence:
The exposure of the
RE extending credit facility against a guarantee shall be treated as a claim /
exposure on the RE which is providing the counter guarantee.
requirements.object: claim / exposure on the RE which is providing the counter guarantee

requirements.full_sentence:
Only genuine trade bills shall be co-accepted, and it shall be ensured that the
goods covered by bills co-accepted are actually received in the stock accounts of
the borrowers.
requirements.object: shall be ensured that the goods covered by bills co-accepted are actually received in the stock accounts of the borrowers

requirements.full_sentence:
Proper records of the bills co-accepted for each customer shall be maintained, so
that the commitments for each customer and the total commitments at a branch
can be readily ascertained, and these shall be part of internal audit.
requirements.object: the commitments for each customer and the total commitments at a branch can be readily ascertained, and these shall be part of

requirements.full_sentence:
The bank shall not co-accept bills drawn by another RE or where the buyer / seller
has received funding for the underlying trade transaction from any RE.
requirements.object: another RE or where the buyer / seller has received funding for the underlying trade transaction from any RE

requirements.full_sentence:
The Banks permitted as Authorized Dealer (AD) may extend NFB facilities as
permitted under the extant regulations / directions issued under Foreign Exchange
Management Act, 1999, for bonafide current or capital account transaction,
including guarantees in respect of debt or other liability incurred by an exporter on
account of exports from India.
requirements.object: permitted under the extant regulations / directions issued under Foreign Exchange Management Act, 1999, for bonafide current

requirements.full_sentence:
Provided that such guarantees shall not be
issued by banks for the purpose of raising loans / advances of any kind by the
foreign entity except in connection with the ordinary course of business overseas.
requirements.object: for the purpose of raising loans / advances of any kind by the foreign entity except in connection with the ordinary course of busi

requirements.full_sentence:
Further while extending such guarantees, banks shall ensure effective monitoring
of the end use of such facilities and its conformity with the business needs of such
entities.
requirements.object: the end use of such facilities and its conformity with the business needs of such entities

requirements.full_sentence:
All extant regulatory prescriptions for credit and investment exposures by the
banks, unless specified otherwise in these Directions, shall continue to apply.

For disclosure of the details of NFB credit facilities, the bank shall be guided by
Reserve Bank of India (Local Area Banks - Financial Statements: Presentation
and Disclosures) Directions, 2025.
requirements.object: Bank of India (Local Area Banks - Financial Statements: Presentation and Disclosures) Directions, 2025

requirements.full_sentence:
FEMA
8/2000-RB dated May 03, 2000;

Notwithstanding paragraph 86 above, bank shall comply with all the related
regulatory norms including Reserve Bank of India (Local Area Banks -
Concentration Risk Management) Directions, 2025.
requirements.object: related regulatory norms including Reserve Bank of India (Local Area Banks - Concentration Risk Management) Directions, 2025

requirements.full_sentence:
The bank shall abide by the following Loan to Value (LTV) and Risk Weights (RWs)
while deciding the quantum of housing loan to be granted:
LTV Ratio
Risk
Category of Loan
(%)
Weight(%)
(1) Individual Housing Loans
requirements.object: Loan to Value (LTV) and Risk Weights (RWs) while deciding the quantum of housing loan to be granted: LTV Ratio Risk Category of

requirements.full_sentence:
Commercial Real Estate - Residential Housing (CRE-RH) shall consist of loans to
builders/developers for residential housing projects (except for captive
consumption) under CRE segment.
requirements.object: builders/developers for residential housing projects (except for captive consumption) under CRE segment

requirements.full_sentence:
Such projects shall ordinarily not include non-
residential commercial real estate.
requirements.object: residential commercial real estate

requirements.full_sentence:
shopping complex, school, etc.) can
also be classified under CRE-RH, provided that the commercial area in the
residential housing projects does not exceed 10 per cent of the total Floor Space
Index (FSI) of the project.
requirements.object: the residential housing projects does not exceed 10 per cent of the total Floor Space Index (FSI) of the project

conditions:
In case the FSI of the commercial area in the
predominantly residential housing complex exceeds the ceiling of 10 per cent
requirements.full_sentence:
In case the FSI of the commercial area in the
predominantly residential housing complex exceeds the ceiling of 10 per cent, the
project loans shall be classified as CRE and not CRE-RH.
requirements.object: and not CRE-RH

requirements.full_sentence:
Banks' exposure to third dwelling unit onwards to an individual shall also be treated
as CRE exposures.
requirements.object: CRE exposures

requirements.full_sentence:
In such
cases, the exposure shall be reckoned for regulatory / prudential exposure limit, if
any, fixed by RBI or by the bank itself, for all the categories to which the exposure
is assigned.
requirements.object: / prudential exposure limit

requirements.full_sentence:
The Directions,
instructions and guidelines already repealed shall continue to remain repealed.

Notwithstanding such repeal, any action taken or purported to have been taken, or
initiated under the repealed Directions, instructions, or guidelines shall continue to
be governed by the provisions thereof.
requirements.object: by the provisions thereof

requirements.full_sentence:
All approvals or acknowledgments granted
under these repealed lists shall be deemed as governed by these Directions.
requirements.object: by these Directions

requirements.full_sentence:
Further, the repeal of these directions, instructions, or guidelines shall not in any
way prejudicially affect:
(1) any right, obligation or liability acquired, accrued, or incurred thereunder.
requirements.object: affect: (1) any right, obligation or liability acquired, accrued, or incurred thereunder

requirements.full_sentence:
and any such investigation, legal proceedings or remedy may be instituted,
continued, or enforced and any such penalty, forfeiture or punishment may be
imposed as if those directions, instructions, or guidelines had not been
repealed.
requirements.object: enforced and any such penalty, forfeiture or punishment may be imposed as if those directions, instructions, or guideline

requirements.full_sentence:
The provisions of these Directions shall be in addition to, and not in derogation of
the provisions of any other laws, rules, regulations, or directions, for the time being
in force.
requirements.object: and not in derogation of the provisions of any other laws, rules, regulations, or directions, for the time being in force

requirements.full_sentence:
For the purpose of giving effect to the provisions of these Directions or in order to
remove any difficulties in the application or interpretation of the provisions of these
Directions, the RBI may, if it considers necessary, issue necessary clarifications in
respect of any matter covered herein and the interpretation of any provision of
these Directions given by the RBI shall be final and binding.
